The premium plan provides genre-specific writing style guidance and citation assistance.

description: Notion AI integrates generative AI directly into the popular all-in-one workspace, helping students draft notes, summarize lengthy readings, and generate structured study plans. It lets you chat with your entire Notion database to quickly surface important information. The free tier is generous for individual students.

The summarizer can condense long journal articles into key bullet points in seconds. It also integrates with Google Docs and Microsoft Word for seamless editing.

description: Cursor is a fork of VS Code built with AI at its core, enabling students to chat with their entire codebase, generate multi-file edits, and fix bugs by describing them in plain English. Its composer feature can scaffold entire applications from a short description. The free Hobby tier provides generous monthly AI completions.

name: Replit AI
tagline: Browser-based coding environment with built-in AI assistant
bestFor: Coding directly in the browser without setup
uniHack: Share a Replit link with classmates to collaborate on coding assignments in real time
description: Replit lets students code in over 50 programming languages directly in the browser without any local setup. Its integrated AI assistant can explain errors, generate boilerplate, and suggest improvements. Real-time multiplayer coding makes it perfect for pair programming on group assignments.

name: Tabnine
tagline: Privacy-first AI code completion for every IDE
bestFor: Code autocomplete with strong privacy controls
uniHack: Enable whole-line completions in your campus-approved IDE for faster coding assignments
description: Tabnine provides AI-powered code completion that runs locally or in the cloud and integrates with virtually every major IDE. Its privacy-first architecture is especially useful at universities with strict data policies. The free tier covers most student needs with accurate single and multi-line suggestions.

name: Codeium
tagline: Free AI coding assistant with autocomplete and chat
bestFor: Free alternative to Copilot for student projects
uniHack: Install Codeium in VS Code for free Copilot-level completions with no credit card required
description: Codeium offers fast, accurate AI code completions and an in-editor chat assistant completely free for individual developers and students. It supports more than 70 programming languages and integrates with VS Code, JetBrains, Neovim, and others. It is a popular choice for students who cannot access paid alternatives.

name: Perplexity AI
tagline: AI search engine with cited real-time answers
bestFor: Getting sourced answers to research questions instantly
uniHack: Use the Focus > Academic mode to restrict results to scholarly sources like arXiv and PubMed
description: Perplexity AI acts as an AI-powered research engine that provides direct answers to questions with citations to real web sources. Students can use its Academic focus mode to surface peer-reviewed papers and academic content. Every answer includes clickable source links, making fact-checking straightforward.

name: Elicit
tagline: AI research assistant for finding and summarizing papers
bestFor: Literature reviews and systematic research synthesis
uniHack: Paste your research question and let Elicit pull the top 20 papers with auto-extracted key findings
description: Elicit is designed for academic research, helping students find relevant papers, extract key data from studies, and map the literature around any topic. Its semantic search goes beyond keyword matching to surface conceptually related work. Researchers and students use it to conduct systematic reviews far faster than manually.

name: Consensus
tagline: Search engine that finds scientific consensus on research questions
bestFor: Quickly finding what scientific evidence says on a topic
uniHack: Ask a yes/no research question to see what percentage of studies support or refute the claim
description: Consensus searches over 200 million scientific papers and aggregates findings into a consensus meter showing how much the research agrees or disagrees on a topic. Students can type a research question in plain English and get evidence-backed summaries instead of raw search results. It is especially useful for health, psychology, and social science topics.

name: Semantic Scholar
tagline: Free AI-powered academic search engine with paper insights
bestFor: Finding and understanding academic papers with AI insights
uniHack: Use the TLDR feature to get a two-sentence summary of any paper before deciding to read the full version
description: Semantic Scholar is a free academic search engine powered by AI that covers over 200 million papers across all fields. Its AI-generated TLDR feature gives students a quick summary of any paper, while citation graphs help them trace how ideas evolve across the literature. Entirely free, making it ideal for budget-conscious students.

name: SciSpace
tagline: AI copilot for reading and understanding research papers
bestFor: Understanding dense research papers with AI explanations
uniHack: Highlight any confusing paragraph and click Ask to get an instant plain-English explanation
description: SciSpace lets students upload research papers or find them via search and then ask the AI copilot questions about the content inline. It can explain complex equations, interpret tables, and suggest related papers for deeper reading. The tool is especially helpful for students entering a new research area who encounter unfamiliar jargon.

name: Connected Papers
tagline: Visual graph of connected research papers for literature exploration
bestFor: Visualizing how papers relate and finding seminal works
uniHack: Start with one key paper to generate a graph and instantly spot the 5 most-cited foundational works
description: Connected Papers generates an interactive visual graph of papers related to a seed paper, showing how research fields branch and connect. Students use it to quickly identify the seminal works in a field and discover derivative research they might have missed. The graph clusters help visualize sub-topics within a research area.

name: Anki
tagline: Spaced repetition flashcard system for long-term memory retention
pricingDetail: Free on desktop and Android; $25 one-time on iOS
bestFor: Memorizing vocabulary, formulas, and facts with proven science
uniHack: Import community decks for your medical school, bar exam, or language class to save card-making time
description: Anki is the gold-standard spaced repetition flashcard app used by medical students, language learners, and professionals worldwide. Its algorithm schedules card reviews at optimal intervals to maximize long-term memory retention with minimum study time. Thousands of community-shared decks cover virtually every subject students need.

name: Quizlet
tagline: AI-powered flashcards and study modes for any subject
bestFor: Creating and studying flashcard sets with AI explanations
uniHack: Use the Q-Chat feature to have the AI quiz you conversationally instead of clicking through cards
description: Quizlet is one of the most popular study platforms among students, combining AI-powered flashcard creation with interactive study modes like Learn, Spell, and Match. The Q-Chat AI tutor engages students conversationally to reinforce concepts. Millions of pre-made sets cover everything from AP exams to university-level courses.

name: Khan Academy Khanmigo
tagline: AI tutor that guides students through problems without giving answers away
bestFor: Math tutoring and concept mastery through guided practice
uniHack: When stuck on a proof, ask Khanmigo to ask you leading questions rather than showing the solution
description: Khanmigo is Khan Academy's GPT-4-powered AI tutor that teaches through guided questioning rather than simply giving answers. It is designed for K-12 through introductory college subjects and is deeply integrated with Khan Academy's curriculum and practice exercises. The platform is free or donation-based, making it one of the most accessible AI tutors available.

name: Photomath
tagline: Snap a math problem and get step-by-step solutions instantly
pricingDetail: Free basic; Plus $10/month for animated explanations
bestFor: Understanding math problem-solving steps from a photo
uniHack: Don't just copy the answer — read through each step to understand the method for your exam
description: Photomath lets students photograph any handwritten or printed math problem and instantly receive detailed step-by-step solutions. It supports arithmetic through calculus and explains each step clearly, helping students understand methods rather than just copying answers. The Plus plan adds animated explanations and expert-created problem walkthroughs.

name: Wolfram Alpha
tagline: Computational knowledge engine for math, science, and more
bestFor: Solving complex math, chemistry, and physics problems computationally
uniHack: Type 'solve x^2+5x+6=0 step by step' to see full derivation instead of just the answer
description: Wolfram Alpha is a computational knowledge engine that can solve calculus problems, balance chemical equations, interpret statistical data, and answer scientific questions with precise, sourced answers. Students in STEM fields rely on it for complex problem-solving beyond basic calculators. Its Pro plan provides detailed step-by-step solutions for studying.

name: Socratic by Google
tagline: AI homework helper that explains concepts visually
bestFor: Getting visual explanations to homework questions via camera
uniHack: Take a photo of your homework question to get curated video explanations linked to the topic
description: Socratic by Google uses AI to identify questions from photos and surface the best explanations, videos, and resources available online. It covers math, science, English, and history for middle and high school students. The visual and video-focused approach suits students who learn better from diagrams than from text.

name: Todoist AI
tagline: Smart task manager with AI natural language scheduling
bestFor: Organizing assignments and deadlines with natural language
uniHack: Type 'submit chemistry lab every Friday at 11pm' and AI will create recurring reminders automatically
description: Todoist uses AI to parse natural language task entries, automatically setting due dates, priorities, and recurring schedules. Students can dump all their assignments in plain text and let the app organize them by priority and deadline. Cross-device sync and calendar integration keep academic schedules consistent everywhere.

name: Motion
tagline: AI scheduler that automatically plans your day around your tasks
bestFor: Auto-scheduling study blocks around classes and deadlines
uniHack: Add all assignments as tasks with deadlines and let Motion block out study time automatically
description: Motion uses AI to automatically build and rebuild your daily schedule based on your tasks, deadlines, meetings, and personal work style. Students can input all upcoming deadlines and the app will allocate focused study blocks across the calendar, automatically rescheduling when conflicts arise. It reduces the cognitive load of planning exam prep periods.

name: Otter.ai

uniHack: Enable Otter on your phone during lecture and review the searchable transcript instead of handwritten notes
description: Otter.ai provides real-time transcription of lectures, study sessions, and video calls, producing searchable, speaker-labeled notes. Its AI generates automatic summaries so students can review key points without rewatching entire recordings. Integrations with Zoom and Google Meet make it easy to capture any online class.

name: Midjourney

description: Midjourney is the leading AI image generation platform known for producing stunning artistic and photorealistic images from text prompts. Students use it to create custom visuals for presentations, posters, and creative projects. The web interface replaced the Discord-only workflow, making it significantly more accessible for everyday use.

name: DALL-E 3

description: DALL-E 3 is OpenAI's image generation model built into ChatGPT that excels at understanding nuanced, complex prompts and generating images that closely match the description. It can render legible text within images, making it useful for creating infographics, posters, and slides. Access through ChatGPT Plus includes up to 50 image generations per day.

name: Adobe Firefly

description: Adobe Firefly is trained on licensed and public domain content, making it safe for academic submissions and commercial student projects without copyright concerns. name: Stable Diffusion

name: Leonardo AI

description: Leonardo AI offers a wide range of fine-tuned image generation models suited for game design, concept art, and creative projects. Its generous daily free token allowance makes it accessible to students on a budget.

description: Tome is an AI presentation tool focused on visual storytelling with flexible tile-based layouts that adapt fluidly to different content types. description: Adobe Express is a streamlined design tool ideal for students creating quick visual content like social media posts, flyers, and academic posters. description: Microsoft Copilot is an AI assistant embedded throughout the Microsoft 365 suite, helping students draft Word documents, analyze Excel data, build PowerPoint decks, and summarize Teams meeting notes. description: Obsidian is a powerful note-taking app that stores notes as local Markdown files and uses bidirectional linking to build a personal knowledge graph. Students use it to connect ideas across courses, creating a permanent research database. Community AI plugins like Copilot for Obsidian enable chatting with an entire vault of notes.

name: Speechify

name: Scite.ai

description: Zotero is the gold-standard free reference manager for academics and students, capable of saving papers from any website and automatically generating formatted bibliographies in APA, MLA, Chicago, or any other style. description: Miro is a leading online collaborative whiteboard used by student teams for brainstorming, project planning, and mind mapping. description: Lumen5 automatically converts blog articles or written scripts into formatted video slideshows using AI to match text with relevant stock footage and visuals. description: DeepL is widely regarded as the most accurate AI translation tool available, producing nuanced translations that preserve academic tone better than most alternatives. description: The Hemingway Editor highlights complex sentences, passive voice, adverbs, and hard-to-read passages in different colors, guiding students to write more clearly and concisely. description: LM Studio is a free desktop application that lets students download and run open-source AI models like Llama and Mistral locally on their own computer without internet or API costs. description: Hugging Face is the central hub for open-source AI models, datasets, and machine learning tools used by students and researchers worldwide.
